perturbation.original_text: 1.2 Term This agreement is for a term of ten (10) years.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 Term This agreement is for an initial term of five (5) years, automatically extending to a total of ten (10) years if the Customer meets specific performance targets detailed in section 13.1.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a condition to the original 10-year term, making it ambiguous. The term now depends on performance targets described later. This contradicts the straightforward declaration of 'ten (10) years' creating ambiguity and making the automatic renewal described on section 1.3 being contradict, since now the term depends of the target and not only the time.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.3 Renewal This agreement will be automatically renewed at the end of every ten (10) year term, with each subsequent term of renewal being for a ten (10) year term. A six (6) months notice must be given by either party of their intention to terminate relations due to any reason other than breach of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated a fixed term of 10 years. The changed text introduces a contingency based on performance targets, altering the term's certainty and contradicting the automatic renewal clause in section 1.3. Section 1.3 states that the agreement will be automatically renewed at the end of every ten year term, but the changed text states the term is initially 5 years, which automatically extends to 10 years if a condition is met. Therefore it is contradictory.
